settings.title: What That Orange Dashboard Light Really Means
settings.excerpt: Not every warning light is an emergency, but one of them genuinely is, and people often pick the wrong one to ignore.
settings.body:
We get a lot of cars in with a light on the dash and a driver who has been ignoring it for weeks. Here is the short version of what to do, because not all of those lights mean the same thing.

The engine light

A flashing engine light means stop soon. Steady orange usually means book it in this week. Flashing means a misfire that can wreck your catalytic converter if you keep driving, and a catalytic converter is one of the most expensive things on the car to replace.

The red oil light

The oil pressure light, the red one shaped like a can, means pull over and switch off now. Running an engine with no oil pressure can end it in minutes, turning what might have been a cheap fix into a new engine.

  • Red lights generally mean stop now
  • Steady orange means book it in soon
  • A flashing orange light is more urgent than a steady one

A useful rule of thumb is colour. Reds are warnings that something needs attention right now, ambers are advisories that something needs looking at before long, and greens or blues are just telling you a system is on. When two lights come on together, treat the more serious one as the one that matters.

Tyre pressure and washer-fluid lights are not emergencies, but the engine and oil lights are not worth a gamble. When in doubt, ring us and describe the colour and whether it flashes. That one detail usually tells us whether it can wait until Thursday or needs you off the road today.
